data.title: Rambus falls after rating cut at Baird
data.description:
Shares of Rambus (RMBS) fell about 18% premarket on Tuesday following first quarter results and a rating cut by Baird Equity.

The firm downgraded Rambus — which offers memory interface chips — to Neutral from Outperform while keeping the price target unchanged at $120.

"While the acceleration in x86 demand, notably driven by agentic AI, is positive for Rambus, we are increasingly seeing the potential for a slowdown in RDIMM YoY growth next year due to increasing DRAM shortages which we expect will persist throughout 2027 (with most of the new DRAM capacity going to HBM), while rocketing DRAM pricing may impact MRDIMM volumes as well. Rambus is the classic case of a unit-driven top-line impacted at times of severe memory shortages without the benefit of higher pricing," said analysts led by Tristan Gerra.

The analysts noted that Rambus remains one of the highest-quality names within their small-cap coverage list.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Tuesday: LLY, UPS, RITM, SPGI
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed before the bell as investors awaited more earnings while U.S.-Iran updates remained in the forefront.

Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

S&P Global (SPGI) was trading 1.01% higher in premarket trade after first-quarter earnings exceeded analyst estimates. The company, known for its S&P 500 index and credit ratings, reported first-quarter adjusted diluted earnings per share of $4.97, up 14% from the prior-year quarter and exceeding the consensus of $4.82. The full-year 2026 revenue guidance was revised lower.

Eli Lilly (LLY) was up 0.38% before the opening bell following news of a research collaboration with Profluent, a biotech firm focused on AI-driven drug discovery. The deal, worth up to $2.25 billion, aims to develop and commercialize genetic medicines. As part of the agreement, Profluent will leverage its AI technologies to design and optimize recombinases, a group of enzymes commonly used in gene editing.

Rithm Capital (RITM) was trading 1.48% higher at $10.27 during premarket trading after the mortgage REIT’s Q1 earnings exceeded analyst expectations. The New York-based company posted Q1 non-GAAP EPS of $0.51, which exceeds the S&P Cap IQ consensus estimate of $0.50 but lags the prior-quarter figure of $0.74. The company recorded growth in mortgage origination and servicing, residential lending, and asset management.

United Parcel Service (UPS) was down 4.54% in premarket trade. The company holds the most attractive valuation among major air freight and logistics stocks, earning an A- valuation grade in Seeking Alpha’s quantitative rankings. The grade measures how a stock is priced relative to its fundamentals, with UPS’s standing reflecting how competitively priced the stock remains relative to its underlying business fundamentals.

data.title: Fuel Tech secures $10M in air pollution control contracts
data.description:
• Fuel Tech (FTEK) announced on Tuesday that it has been awarded multiple air pollution control (APC) contracts valued at approximately $10M with domestic utility and industrial customers.
  • The awards include a contract to integrate Fuel Tech’s selective catalytic reduction (SCR) technology with two new natural gas-fired turbines for a publicly owned Midwest municipal utility, as well as two orders from existing industrial customers for SNCR and SCR upgrades. 
  • The largest award calls for integration of Fuel Tech’s SCR pollution control systems with two new gas turbines that will increase the municipal utility’s plant output by approximately 100 MW.
  • The expanded generating station is expected to become operational in 2029. Fuel Tech indicated it will commence engineering work immediately and that equipment deliveries are scheduled to begin in late 2027.

data.title: Apple's Q2 results may offer 'slight' upside, guidance may be a positive surprise: UBS
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is set to report fiscal second-quarter results on April 30, and investment firm UBS is expecting a “slight” upside to the consensus, based on Mac and iPhone-related strength.

“Supply chain checks and analysis of sell-through data suggest modest upside to our iPhone ests while demand for the Neo and Mac mini suggests demand at least in-line with our Mac rev forecast,” UBS analyst David Vogt wrote in a note to clients. Vogt reiterated his Neutral rating on Apple but raised his price target to $287 from $280.

Apple shares rose fractionally in premarket trading on Tuesday, bucking the broader tech sell-off.

Turning to guidance, Vogt said he is expecting a “solid” offering for the June quarter and upped his earnings estimates as a result.

“Rising memory prices and Apple's ability to secure adequate supply is driving smartphone share gains for the iPhone product line at the expense of the broader Android ecosystem in our view,” Vogt explained. “In addition, solid demand for the Mac portfolio in part driven by initial success of the lower priced 'Neo' as well as demand for the 'mini' increasingly used to run AI agents like OpenClaw should drive incremental upside relative to our expectations.”

A consensus of analysts expects Apple to earn $1.94 per share on $109.73B in revenue for the fiscal second quarter.

data.title: WTI crude tops $100 as Trump reportedly unhappy with Iran's proposal to open Hormuz
data.description:
U.S. crude oil futures jumped above $100/bbl Tuesday, gaining more than 4%, following reports that President Trump is dissatisfied with Iran’s proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for June delivery up 4.1% to $100.32/bbl, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for June delivery up 3.4% to $111.94/bbl, after both benchmarks gained more than 2% on Monday.

Iran has offered to reopen Hormuz if the U.S. lifts its naval blockade and pledges no further attacks, but it wants to postpone discussions on its nuclear program.

Even if hostilities ended immediately, a return to normal market conditions would take months, according to Andy Lipow, president of Lipow Oil Associates, citing the need to clear mines, ease tanker congestion, and gradually restart production and refining.

Lipow told CNBC it likely would take 4-6 months for oil markets to stabilize, after factoring in shipping and distribution lags, with prices remaining elevated in the interim.

Separately, the United Arab Emirates said it has decided to exit OPEC and OPEC+ as of May 1.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (XLE)

data.title: Shimmick appoints new COO
data.description:
• Shimmick (SHIM) named Sarah Tacker as executive VP and chief operating officer, effective April 28, 2026.
  • Tacker joins from FlatironDragados, where she led Northern California operations.
  • SHIM shares down 5.6% premarket.

data.title: Itron Non-GAAP EPS of $1.49 beats by $0.25, revenue of $587M beats by $14.86M
data.description:
• Itron press release (ITRI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.49 beats by $0.25.
  • Revenue of $587M (-3.3% Y/Y) beats by $14.86M.

  • Total first quarter revenue of $587 million compared to $607 million in the prior year. The decrease was driven primarily by portfolio optimization and the timing of project deployments.

    Device Solutions revenue decreased 1%, or 9% in constant currency, due to lower legacy electricity product sales related to portfolio optimization in EMEA and lower North American project deployments.

    Networked Solutions revenue decreased 13%, or 14% in constant currency, due to the timing of project deployments.

    Outcomes revenue increased 22%, or 20% in constant currency, due to increased recurring and services revenue.

    Resiliency Solutions revenue was $16 million which now includes revenue from both Urbint and Locusview. The Locusview acquisition closed in January 2026.

  • Outlook for the second quarter of 2026 is as follows:

    • Revenue between $560 and $570 million vs $606.97M consensus
    • Non-GAAP diluted EPS between $1.25 and $1.35 vs $1.46 consensus
  • Shares -3% PM.

data.title: Franklin Resources Non-GAAP EPS of $0.71 beats by $0.16, revenue of $2.29B beats by $240M
data.description:
• Franklin Resources press release (BEN): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.71 beats by $0.16.
  • Revenue of $2.29B (+8.5% Y/Y) beats by $240M.
  • Total AUM was $1,682.1 billion at March 31, 2026, down $1.9 billion during the quarter due to the negative impact of $30.2 billion of net market change, distributions, and other, partially offset by $16.9 billion of long-term net inflows, inclusive of $4.1 billion of long-term net outflows at Western, and $11.4 billion of cash management net inflows. Long-term net inflows for the quarter include $3.2 billion of long-term reinvested distributions.
  • Shares +2.6% PM.

data.title: Centene raises outlook as declining medical costs drive Q1 beat
data.description:
Centene (CNC) raised its full-year outlook on Tuesday after the managed care firm reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 results thanks to a decline in its health benefits ratio, a closely followed industry benchmark indicating the level of medical expenses borne by a health insurer.

For the quarter, the Saint Louis, Missouri firm, which mainly focuses on Medicaid, generated $3.37 of adjusted earnings per share on $49.9B in revenue with ~7% YoY growth and ~16% YoY growth, respectively, exceeding the consensus by $2.4B and $1.24.

Its health benefits ratio, which calculates the proportion of premiums paid out for medical care, fell 20 bps from the prior year period to reach 87.3%, compared to the 89.2% projected by analysts, according to Bloomberg data.

For the quarter, Centene (CNC) recorded a 50 bps drop in its Medicaid HBR, with the company attributing the gain to rate and revenue increases, its management of medical costs, and a drop in flu-related expenses.

While its Medicaid revenue rose ~6% YoY to $23.6B, revenue from its commercial business fell ~6% YoY to $9.6B, and its Medicare operations added ~$10.3B to the topline with ~18% YoY growth.

Looking ahead, the company raised its full-year revenue and adjusted EPS outlook to $187.5B - $191.5B and more than $3.40, compared to $188.8B and more than $3.02 projected by analysts, respectively. Previously, Centene (CNC) forecasted adding more than $3.00 of adjusted EPS on $186.5B - $190.5B revenue for this year.
